summary: Iteration 4 frontend re-test of the NERV-OS terminal redesign on the public preview URL. 13/14 review-request scenarios PASS. Boot sequence works (skip-intro-btn dismisses, localStorage 'nerv_boot_done_v1' persists). All 9 sections render with the new dense terminal aesthetic — DOM contains 9 .term-section nodes, 55 .prompt nodes, 56 .row-rec rows, 9 .ascii-box panels, 13 .line-numbers nodes — zero React/console errors. Hero shows DOSSIER terminal with NERV ASCII banner, MAGI hex tree, harmonics waveform, vitals telemetry grid, warning buffer log, active time clock. Personnel renders as NERV DOSSIER (orange) with ID chip, EDU table rows, biography/aspirations entries, FN log and REDACTED block. Operations renders as MAGI SQL-style table; OP-001/002/003 row testids present; clicking [data-testid='operation-OP-001'] opens [data-testid='operation-modal']. Skills SYNCHRONIZATION DIAGNOSTICS has LANGUAGES/FRAMEWORKS/TOOLS/CREATIVE/RESEARCH groups with 27 row-recs containing PROBE/NAME/WAVEFORM/SYNC%/STATE columns. Economics SEELE terminal renders (red, monoliths panel, vote tally, 5 row-rec paper records). Achievements renders as NERV RECORDS audit log. Status LIVE TELEMETRY with PROBE|VALUE|WAVEFORM|STATE rows. Gallery VISUAL ARCHIVE renders. Contact COMMS TERMINAL (green) with comms.log stream + form fields contact-name/contact-email/contact-message/contact-submit. Nav testids nav-home..nav-contact all present; mode-toggle adds pro-mode class; sound-toggle, resume-download present; nerv-logo-btn x10 triggers [data-testid='third-impact']; backquote(~) opens [data-testid='secret-terminal']; Konami code opens [data-testid='seele-overlay']; terminate-session opens fullscreen [data-testid='ending-screen'] with 'SESSION TERMINATED.' (verified visually). All external imgur images (TkOO9fF.jpg 704x480 x2 refs, 6mDPV0L.jpg 704x480 x2 refs) and customer-assets images (umbz8vym 637x358, h91tlhtk 896x337) load with non-zero naturalWidth. Zero pageerrors, zero console errors throughout.

ui_bugs.issue: Modal close button [data-testid='close-modal'] is unclickable via real mouse clicks AND the modal backdrop click-to-dismiss is also blocked in the top ~56px of the viewport. The fixed Navigation header (z-50) visually renders on top of the modal (z-[200]) — document.elementFromPoint at the close button center (855.9, 30.75) returns the nav-economics button instead of close-modal. JS programmatic .click() on close-modal correctly fires setActive(null) and closes the modal, so the React handler is wired; the failure is a stacking/hit-testing issue. Visual confirmation: in modal screenshot the Navigation bar (NERV logo + 9 nav links + SND/MODE/REPORT toggles) renders at full opacity over the top of the modal's backdrop and term-header. Likely root cause: another fixed/sticky overlay (HudOverlay scanlines / TerminalScreen chrome / a CSS filter establishing a stacking context outside the modal subtree) is being painted on top, OR a CSS rule is reordering paint despite z-index values. Also note: there is no Escape-key handler on the modal — pressing Esc does not close it either, so users whose viewport places the close-X under the nav have no keyboard fallback.

integration_issues.issue: MOCKED — Web3Forms access_key remains a placeholder (carried over from iteration 2/3). UI submit path renders fine but no real transmission occurs. Not re-validated end-to-end this round.

action_items: Fix modal close button hit-area: ensure Navigation (z-50) is truly painted below the modal (z-[200]) — investigate any ancestor establishing a stacking context (filters, transforms, will-change, isolation, mix-blend-mode) on the Navigation header or App root that elevates it above z:200 children. Easiest belt-and-suspenders fixes: (a) hide/dim the Navigation while a modal is open (conditional render), (b) raise modal to a Portal mounted on document.body so no ancestor can interfere, or (c) lift modal z-index to a very high value AND add isolation:isolate on the modal root.
Add Escape-key handler to operation-modal as a keyboard fallback (useEffect with keydown listener that calls setActive(null)).
Optional: replace Web3Forms placeholder access_key with a real key OR document that the contact form is intentionally a UI-only demo.
critical_code_review_comments: OperationsSection.jsx line 134 — modal mounted inline inside the section instead of a React Portal; this means parent stacking contexts (Navigation header, HudOverlay, App wrapper) can affect paint order. Portaling to document.body would eliminate the close-button overlap bug entirely.
OperationsSection.jsx — modal lacks an Escape-key handler and an aria-modal/role='dialog'/focus-trap. Even after stacking is fixed, this is a basic a11y miss.

Multiple .term-section renderers (9 instances) each may run their own setInterval/RAF (carried review note from iteration_2 about MagiHexDisplay/HarmonicsGraph) — consider pausing off-screen timers via IntersectionObserver to reduce idle CPU.
Web3Forms placeholder access_key still mocked — same pre-existing item from iterations 2/3.

success_rate.frontend: ~93% (13/14 scenarios — modal-close interaction failed under real mouse clicks; all other rendering, navigation, easter-egg, and asset-load scenarios passed)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 4 redesign verified — every section uses TerminalScreen wrapper and renders dense .term-section/.prompt/.row-rec/.ascii-box markup. Counts to assert: 9 term-sections, ~55 prompts, ~56 row-recs, 9 ascii-boxes. All data-testids from iterations 1-3 are preserved. One open bug: modal close hit-area overlapped by Navigation header (stacking issue). Programmatic JS click on [data-testid='close-modal'] still works — so React state wiring is correct, only paint/hit-testing is wrong. Easter-egg overlays (third-impact/secret-terminal/seele-overlay) can leave residue if not dismissed; always reload between independent overlay tests. terminate-session triggers a true fullscreen ending-screen (verified). Boot gate is the localStorage key 'nerv_boot_done_v1' = 'true'.
rca of the issue: Bug: Close button on [data-testid='operation-modal'] is unclickable via real mouse events. Reproduction: 1) Load app, dismiss boot (or set localStorage nerv_boot_done_v1=true and reload). 2) Scroll to operations section, click [data-testid='operation-OP-001']. 3) Modal opens. 4) Click [data-testid='close-modal'] with a real mouse/Playwright click — nothing happens. 5) Verify via document.elementFromPoint(855.9, 30.75): returns the Navigation 'nav-economics' button, not the close button. 6) JS .click() on the close-modal element correctly closes the modal, proving the onClick handler is wired. Root cause: although the modal root has z-[200] and the Navigation header has z-50, the Navigation header is being painted (and hit-tested) ON TOP of the modal in the top ~56px of the viewport. Most likely cause is a stacking-context boundary somewhere in the ancestor chain (an ancestor of the modal has transform/filter/backdrop-filter/will-change/isolation/mix-blend-mode that creates a stacking context, capping the modal's effective z-index below the Navigation's stacking context). Mitigation (recommended): render the modal through a React Portal to document.body (escapes all ancestor stacking contexts), add `isolation: isolate` on the modal root, and add an Escape-key handler. As a quick alternative, conditionally hide/disable pointer-events on the Navigation header while any modal/overlay is open.
